# priority: high
# category: bugs
** OPEN #128 Auto-generated enum clone in ~initer.til~ is shallow for managed payloads

The delete side of this issue is fixed ([[./126.org][#126]], now in [[../fixed/][issues/fixed/]]):
  ~generate_enum_delete_method~ recurses into payload-owned data.

  Remaining problem: auto-generated enum ~clone~ does NOT deep-clone
  managed payloads. In ~generate_enum_clone_method~ (initer.til:668),
  the payload is declared with ~own_type = OwnType.Ref~ and passed
  directly to the constructor without calling ~.clone()~. This means
  the cloned enum shares the same payload data as the original --
  a shallow copy instead of a deep clone.

  This matters for payload enums carrying managed values like:
  - ~Str~
  - structs with owned internals
  - containers such as ~Vec~

  Verified examples already allowed by the language/tests:
  - ~Token.Name: Str~
  - ~Shape.Dot: Point~
  - ~Shape.Line: Str~

  Desired end state:
  - auto-generated enum ~clone~ calls ~PayloadType.clone()~ on managed
    payload data before passing it to the constructor
  - no double-free or aliasing from cloned enums sharing payload buffers
